skill.name: reasoning-dialectical
skill.description: Synthesize opposing views into an integrated decision using thesis, antithesis, synthesis, explicit trade-offs, and stakeholder agreement.
skill.summary: Resolve opposing stakeholder positions through structured dialectical reasoning.
skill.author: BellaBe

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported command execution, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns, but review found these are false positives from Markdown code fences and ordinary prose in SKILL.md. No executable scripts, shell commands, network access, environment access, filesystem mutation, or prompt injection attempts were found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Fences Flagged as Commands
low_findings.description: The external command detections occur on Markdown fence delimiters and YAML examples. They are documentation examples, not shell execution or Ruby backtick execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines contain Markdown fence markers or example blocks only. There is no executable code path, command name, user input, or shell invocation.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Prose Flagged as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography detections appear in the skill description and reasoning examples. No cryptographic API, hash function, cipher, credential handling, or security implementation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding content is explanatory writing about dialectical reasoning. I found no evidence of MD5, DES, SHA1, or any cryptographic operation.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Prose Flagged as Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance detections occur in ordinary decision-making text and examples. No system discovery command, host inspection, process listing, or data collection behavior is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matched lines describe stakeholders, integration, and reasoning concepts. No evidence found of commands such as uname, whoami, ps, id, or environment probing.

content.value_statement: Teams often stall when two valid positions conflict. This skill turns disagreement into a structured thesis, antithesis, and synthesis with explicit trade-offs.
actual_capabilities: Structures a first position as a thesis with stakeholder, evidence, risks, and benefits.
Builds a strong antithesis that challenges assumptions and represents an opposing stakeholder.
Creates an integrated synthesis using integration, sequencing, segmentation, reframing, or transcendence.
Documents trade-offs, mitigations, stakeholder acceptance, actions, metrics, and review timing.
Applies quality gates to avoid weak opposition, false compromise, and abstract conclusions.
limitations: It does not verify the truth of evidence supplied by the user.
It is less useful when there is no genuine tension between positions.
It does not replace stakeholder approval or formal governance.
It may need another reasoning mode for root-cause analysis or historical evaluation.

use_cases.title: Align Product and Sales Priorities
use_cases.description: Compare revenue needs against product learning needs, then produce an integrated roadmap decision.

use_cases.title: Resolve Technical Investment Debates
use_cases.description: Balance platform work against feature delivery with explicit trade-offs and measurable next steps.

use_cases.title: Prepare Executive Decision Briefs
use_cases.description: Turn competing strategic options into a clear synthesis for leadership review.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Position Synthesis
prompt_templates.scenario: Two teams disagree on a decision.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use dialectical reasoning to compare these two positions: [position A] and [position B]. Identify the thesis, antithesis, synthesis, trade-offs, and next actions.

prompt_templates.title: Stakeholder Trade-Off Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Each position has a different stakeholder.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this disagreement with the dialectical framework. For each stakeholder, state their concern, evidence, incentives, risks, and what they must accept in the synthesis.

prompt_templates.title: Strategic Reframing
prompt_templates.scenario: The disagreement may be based on a false choice.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use dialectical reasoning to test whether this is a false dichotomy: [decision context]. Reframe the tension if needed and propose an integrated position.

prompt_templates.title: Implementation-Ready Synthesis
prompt_templates.scenario: A decision needs actions, metrics, and review points.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a full dialectical analysis for [decision]. Include steel-manned thesis and antithesis, synthesis type, explicit losses, mitigations, stakeholder agreement, actions, metrics, and review date.

output_examples.input: Engineering wants a six-month platform rebuild. Sales wants Q2 features for active deals.
output_examples.output: The synthesis recommends an incremental rebuild while delivering the top Q2 features. Engineering accepts a longer rebuild timeline, and Sales accepts prioritizing the most valuable deals.

output_examples.input: Leadership is split between enterprise focus and SMB growth.
output_examples.output: The result frames SMB as the learning engine and enterprise as the revenue engine. It defines feature graduation criteria and metrics for both segments.

output_examples.input: Support wants more reliability work, while Growth wants onboarding experiments.
output_examples.output: The synthesis sequences reliability fixes before high-traffic experiments. It protects experiment velocity with a smaller test plan and review metrics.

best_practices: Provide real stakeholder names, incentives, and evidence before asking for synthesis.
Ask the model to steel-man both sides before choosing a resolution type.
Require explicit trade-offs, owners, metrics, and a review date.
anti_patterns: Do not use it to make one side win without addressing the other concern.
Do not treat a vague compromise as a completed synthesis.
Do not skip evidence quality when the decision depends on factual claims.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It helps an AI assistant synthesize opposing positions into an integrated decision with trade-offs and next steps.

faq.question: When should I use it?
faq.answer: Use it when stakeholders disagree, both sides have merit, and a practical resolution is needed.

faq.question: Does it choose the winning argument?
faq.answer: No. It tries to preserve the valid concern in each position and create a higher-order resolution.

faq.question: Can it verify supporting evidence?
faq.answer: No. It organizes and tests the evidence you provide, but it does not independently verify facts.

faq.question: What should I provide in the prompt?
faq.answer: Provide the decision context, each position, stakeholders, evidence, constraints, and desired decision deadline.

faq.question: Is it safe for marketplace publication?
faq.answer: Yes. The reviewed file contains reasoning instructions and examples only, with no executable behavior found.
